question_text: Explain why a gas expands to fill a container of any size, while a solid maintains its shape.
correct_answer: In a gas, particles are very far apart and move quickly in all directions with little attractive force between them, so they spread throughout the container. In a solid, particles are tightly packed and held in fixed positions by strong attractive forces, so the shape is fixed.
explanation: This explanation uses the particle model to show how particle spacing and movement, combined with the strength of intermolecular forces, determine whether a substance is compressible and maintains shape.
